upgradeCard.title: Upgrade to Pro
upgradeCard.subtitle: Unlock higher limits and premium features for your company.

social.attachNoUrl: That image has no shareable URL — pick one from a public bucket.
social.attachPickerDesc: Pick from your team's media or upload new ones.
social.repostDialogDescription: Add an optional quote and share with your team.
social.deleteConfirmDesc: This can't be undone. Replies stay but the post is hidden.
social.emptyDescription: Be the first to share something with your team. Posts only appear to members of this company.
social.commentsTitle: {count, plural, =0 {No replies} one {# reply} other {# replies}}
social.activityNoSharedPosts: No posts you can see yet.
social.activitySignInPrompt: Sign in to see shared activity.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noDomains: Add your first domain to get started

accessTokens.title: Access Tokens
accessTokens.description: Access tokens allow external applications to interact with your company's resources via API.
accessTokens.createDescription: This token grants full access to your company's API. Store it securely.
accessTokens.name: Name
accessTokens.expiresHint: Leave empty for a token that never expires.
accessTokens.revokeDesc: "{name}" will be permanently revoked. Any application using this token will lose access.
accessTokens.emptyDescription: Create an access token to allow external applications to use the API.
accessTokens.tokenCreatedDesc: Copy your token now. You will not be able to see it again.

team.title: Team
team.name: Name
team.removeConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this member?
team.removeConfirmDesc: {name} will be removed from the team and lose access to all company resources. This cannot be undone.
team.userNotFound: User not found with this email
team.invitationCreatedNoEmail: Invitation created for {email} but email was not sent ({reason}). Configure system mail in Admin → System mail.
team.transferDesc: Transfer this company to {name}. You'll become an admin. A confirmation code will be emailed to you.
team.becomeAdminNote: The member becomes the owner and you become an admin. This can't be undone without another transfer.
team.codeSent: Confirmation code sent to your email
team.transferCodeNotSent: Transfer started but the email couldn't be sent. Configure system mail in Admin → System mail.
team.transferCodeSentDesc: Enter the 6-digit code we emailed you to confirm.
team.inboxAllMailboxesHint: Grants access to every mailbox in the company.
team.inboxAllAccessNote: All mailboxes granted — individual selections are irrelevant.
team.adminHasAllNote: Admin role already covers every permission. No further selection needed.
team.ownerHasAllNote: The owner role holds all permissions implicitly.
team.inviteMemberDesc: Invite a new member and set their permissions.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.emptyDescription: You'll see new mail activity here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.companyNameHint: Display name shown in the team switcher and headers.
settings.companyDescriptionHint: Short bio shown on the company profile page.
settings.slugHint: Used in the URL. Lowercase letters, numbers and single hyphens.
settings.identityDescription: How this workspace appears to members and in URLs.
settings.name: Name
settings.avatarDescription: Shown in the team switcher and dashboard cards.
settings.avatarHint: Square works best. PNG, JPG, WebP or SVG, max 5MB.
settings.avatarPickerDesc: Pick an existing image from this company's media or upload a new one.
settings.coverHint: Wide hero image shown at the top of the company profile. 6:1 aspect, JPG or PNG.
settings.coverPickerDesc: Pick a wide image from this company's media or upload a new one.
settings.mediaPickNoUrl: Could not resolve a URL for this file. Try a public image or re-upload.
settings.auditDescription: Recent actions taken on this workspace — visible to owners and admins.
settings.dangerZoneDescription: Destructive actions for this company. These cannot be undone.
settings.deleteCompanyDescription: Permanently delete this company and everything in it — domains, mailboxes, API keys and members.
settings.deleteCompanyDialogDescription: This action cannot be undone. The following will be permanently removed:
settings.deleteItemDomains: {count, plural, one {# domain} other {# domains}} and their DNS/DKIM configuration
settings.deleteItemMailboxes: {count, plural, one {# mailbox} other {# mailboxes}} and all stored mail
settings.deleteItemMembers: {count, plural, one {# member} other {# members}} will lose access
settings.deleteItemApiKeys: All API keys and access tokens will be revoked

common.noPermission: You don't have permission to access this page

companySelection.title: Choose company
companySelection.subtitle: Pick a company to continue or create a new one.
companySelection.noCompaniesTitle: You don't have any companies yet
companySelection.noCompaniesDesc: Create your first company to get started.
companySelection.createDialogDesc: Give your new company a name. Slug is generated automatically.

profile.title: Profile
profile.passkeysCardDescription: Manage the passkeys (TouchID, security keys, etc.) that can sign you in.
profile.avatarHint: Pick an existing image or upload a new one to one of your buckets.
profile.avatarPickerDesc: Choose any image from your buckets — the public URL is stored on your profile.
profile.avatarPickerNoCompany: Create a company first to upload media.
profile.mediaPickNoUrl: Could not resolve a URL for this file. Try a public image or re-upload.
profile.publicProfileDesc: Share a LinkedIn-style page at /profile/u/{slug}. Toggle visibility off to keep it private.
profile.profileSlugHint: 3–64 characters: lowercase letters, digits, dashes. Leave empty to disable the public link.
profile.headline: Headline
profile.bioPlaceholder: A short description of who you are and what you do.
profile.isPublicProfileHint: When enabled, anyone with the URL can view your name, headline, bio, location, website, and cover image. Email and security info are never exposed.
profile.socialLinksHint: Up to 12. Shown as icons in your profile header and as a Connect list below.
profile.socialLinksEmpty: No social links yet — add the first one.
profile.name: Name
profile.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
profile.deleteAccountDescription: Permanently delete your account and all associated data.
profile.sessionsDescription: Devices that are currently signed in to your account.
profile.revokeAllDesc: Sign out every other device. Your current session will remain active.
profile.twoFactorDesc: Add an extra layer of security using a TOTP authenticator app.
profile.twoFactorDisableDesc: Enter your password to confirm. You will no longer be asked for a code at sign-in.
profile.twoFactorPasswordHint: Enter your current password to start the setup.
profile.twoFactorScanHint: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code.
profile.twoFactorBackupHint: Save these codes in a safe place. Each can be used once to recover your account if you lose access to your authenticator.
profile.linkedAccountsDesc: Connect multiple providers to sign in to the same account.
profile.confirmUnlinkDesc: You will no longer be able to sign in with {provider}.
profile.cannotUnlinkLast: You must keep at least one sign-in method to access your account.
profile.setPasswordDesc: Create a password so you can also sign in with email and password.
profile.deleteAccountCodeSent: We sent a 6-digit code to your email. Enter it below to permanently delete your account.
profile.deleteAccountOwnedWarning: {count} company/companies you own will be permanently deleted with all their data:

appPicker.title: Open an app
appPicker.description: Choose a product to continue with this company.

mail.name: Mail
mail.description: Send and receive email, manage domains and templates.

storage.name: Storage
storage.description: Upload, organize, and serve files across buckets.

admin.name: Admin
admin.description: Platform-wide settings, users, plans, system health.

vault.name: Env Vault
vault.description: Centrally manage environment variables across all your deploys.

auth.name: OAuth clients
auth.description: Let your users sign in with their Sentroy account — register OAuth/OIDC clients per app.

status.name: Status
status.description: Host your own Atlassian Statuspage-style real-time status page on Sentroy.

studio.name: Studio
studio.description: Browser-based DJ and music studio — load tracks, mix, record live sets.

whatsapp.name: WhatsApp Santral
whatsapp.description: Link a WhatsApp number and manage chats, contacts and history from the panel.

linear.name: Linear Lite
linear.description: Triage requests and manage Linear issues — list, kanban and metrics in one panel.

opencut.name: Video Editor
opencut.description: Browser-based video editor — trim, cut, and produce video content right in your browser.

meet.name: Meet
meet.description: Start branded video meetings — invite your team and join from the browser.

list.subtitle: Auth-as-a-Service
list.title: Auth Projects
list.lede: Host your application's user pool on Sentroy. Sign-up, login, JWT issue, refresh, email verification and password reset — Firebase Auth-style with one SDK.
list.emptyBody: No Auth Projects yet. Create your first one to test Sentroy as a Firebase Auth alternative.
list.deleteConfirmDescription: All users, sessions, tokens and audit records will be removed. {count} users will be deleted. This is irreversible.

create.dialogDescription: A new user pool — for sign-up, login, JWT flows. The slug is used as the public route segment (auth.sentroy.com/p/<slug>).
create.slugHint: Lowercase, digits, hyphens. Used in public URLs; cannot be changed afterwards.
create.brandingHint: Used as your brand identity in mail templates and landing pages.
create.verifyHint: When on, users cannot log in until they click the verification link after sign-up.
create.originsHint: Origins that may call the auth API from a browser. Leave empty for server-to-server only (browser CORS denied).

apiKeyReveal.dialogDescription: A master API key was created for {name}. The plaintext is shown only in this window; only its hash is stored after closing. Save it in your RP backend env (e.g. SENTROY_AUTH_API_KEY).
apiKeyReveal.label: API key
apiKeyReveal.infoNote: Heads-up: don't put this key in the browser. Use it server-side from your RP backend (proxy to Sentroy). If leaked, rotate it from the project detail page.

overview.freeLimitWarn: You're approaching the free tier limit — upgrade to Pro for unlimited MAU.
overview.endpointsHint: All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer aps_… (verify-email + password-reset/confirm are exceptions — they authenticate via the single-use token from the email link).
overview.jwksHint: This doc is published at /jwks.json; your backend uses this key to verify access tokens. The kid is an RFC 7638 thumbprint.
overview.rotateJwtDescription: Generates a new RSA 2048-bit keypair. The old key moves to the grace slot and is still published in JWKS — clients with cached JWKS can still verify until you clear it. After the grace window, use "Remove previous key".
overview.previousJwtHint: Published for clients still holding the old JWKS cache.
overview.clearPreviousDescription: Tokens signed with this key will be rejected after this. Make sure your relying parties have fetched the new JWKS (usually 24 hours is plenty).

users.empty: No users yet. They will appear here when your RP calls the signup endpoint.
users.deleteConfirmDescription: {email} and all their sessions will be permanently deleted.
users.revokeConfirmDescription: All sessions for {email} will be ended. Active access tokens auto-expire within 1 hour, but refresh tokens will fail immediately after this action.
users.importDialogDescription: Header row required. Leave password empty to generate a random one — the user can set their own via password reset. Imported users are created as verified.

invite.title: Invite user
invite.description: Sends an invitation link valid for 7 days. The invited user sets a password and creates the account.
invite.hint: Invitation link is valid for 7 days. Only works for emails not yet registered.

emails.description: Default template used for each event — customize to match your brand. With no override, Sentroy renders the default branded template.
emails.variablesHint: Use {key} format inside subject and body.
emails.resetConfirmDescription: Removes your custom template for this event and falls back to Sentroy default.

webhooks.description: RP receivers for auth events. HMAC-SHA256 signed POSTs.
webhooks.emptyBody: Add your receiver URL — HMAC-signed POSTs fire on signup/login/password-changed/locked/deleted events. Verify the X-Sentroy-Signature header in your receiver.
webhooks.createDialogDescription: HTTPS URL recommended. Leave topics empty to subscribe to everything.
webhooks.topicsHint: If none selected, all events are dispatched.
webhooks.deleteConfirmDescription: This webhook will stop receiving events. Cannot be undone.
webhooks.rotateSecretConfirmDescription: Generates a new HMAC secret. Existing receivers won't verify with the new secret — manual update required. Signature failures will be rejected.
webhooks.secretShownDescription: Paste this into your receiver. It cannot be recovered later — if lost, rotate the secret.

settings.enabledHint: When disabled all public auth API endpoints return 404 — your RPs will see API errors.
settings.verifyHint: When on, users cannot log in until they click the link in the verification email.
settings.originsHint: Origins that may call the auth API from a browser. Leave empty for server-to-server only.
settings.socialHint: Tap a provider below to enter credentials. Client ID is public; client secret is AES-256-GCM encrypted. Add the callback URL to your provider allow-list.
settings.clientSecretRotateLabel: Client secret (saved — leave empty to keep, paste new to rotate)
settings.clientSecretRotatePlaceholder: Paste new secret to rotate; otherwise existing is kept
settings.googleProviderHint: Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client ID. Add the callback URL above to "Authorized redirect URIs".
settings.githubProviderHint: github.com → Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps → New. Paste the callback URL above into "Authorization callback URL".
settings.facebookProviderHint: developers.facebook.com → Apps → Add Product → Facebook Login → Settings. Add the callback URL above to "Valid OAuth Redirect URIs".
settings.microsoftProviderHint: Azure Portal → App registrations → New → Web → set "Redirect URI" to the callback above. Then Certificates & secrets → New client secret.
settings.microsoftTenantHint: Leave empty for "common" — any Microsoft account (personal + work). To restrict to your Entra tenant only, enter the tenant UUID.
settings.twitterProviderHint: developer.x.com → Projects & Apps → User authentication settings → set "Callback URI / Redirect URL" to the callback above. App permissions: Read.
settings.twitterEmailWarning: X standard OAuth scopes don't return email. Sentroy uses `username@x.local` placeholder; the user can set a real email via /me/email/change-request.
settings.appleProviderHint: Apple Developer → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles → Services IDs → New (com.example.signin) → enable Sign In with Apple + Configure → domain + Return URL. Then Keys → New → Sign In with Apple → download the .p8 file.
settings.applePrivateKeyRotateLabel: Private key (saved — paste new or leave empty)
settings.applePrivateKeyRotatePlaceholder: Paste new p8 PEM to rotate; otherwise existing is kept
settings.applePrivateKeyHint: Apple Developer → Keys → contents of the downloaded .p8 file (starts with "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----"). Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted. client_secret JWT (ES256) is signed at runtime.
settings.claimsHint: Copy fields from user.metadata into the access token JWT, or add static claims. Reserved (sub/iss/aud/iat/exp/email/name/picture) cannot be overridden.
settings.fromMetadataHint: Comma- or newline-separated. Top-level keys only — nested objects not supported.
settings.staticClaimsHint: Added to every token. Only string/number/boolean values.

apiKey.prefixHint: The plaintext key is hashed in the DB — shown only once on create and rotate. Use this prefix to identify the key in audit logs.
apiKey.rotateHint: If the key was leaked or your plan changed, rotate it. The old key is invalidated immediately — your production RPs will experience brief downtime.
apiKey.rotateConfirmDescription: The current API key becomes invalid immediately — every system using the old key in your RP backend env will get 401. The new key is shown only once; do not close the dialog before deploying it.
apiKey.newDialogDescription: The old key was invalidated. The new key is shown once in this window — once closed it cannot be retrieved.

oauthClients.title: OAuth clients
oauthClients.subtitle: Sentroy Auth
oauthClients.description: Register your sites here so users can sign in with their Sentroy account. Each client gets a {clientIdCode} and {clientSecretCode} — the secret is shown once, copy it into your app's deploy env.
oauthClients.empty: No OAuth clients yet. Create one to add "Sign in with Sentroy" to your site.
oauthClients.editDialogDescription: Update the redirect URIs, scopes, name, or description. The client_id and client_secret stay the same — apps using this client need no changes.
oauthClients.rotateConfirmDescription: The current client_secret will be invalidated immediately. Update SENTROY_CLIENT_SECRET on every deploy using this client. The new secret will be shown once.
oauthClients.deleteConfirmDescription: This deletes OAuth client {clientId}. Sites using it will stop being able to sign users in. Cannot be undone.
oauthClients.secretShownOnceHint: (shown once at create — rotate to get a new one)
oauthClients.createDialogDescription: Register a site that will let users sign in with their Sentroy account. The client_secret will be shown once after creation.
oauthClients.name: Name
oauthClients.redirectUrisHint: Authorization requests with a redirect_uri not on this list are rejected. Add your dev + prod URLs.
oauthClients.scopesPickerHint: Authorize requests for scopes outside this list are rejected. Pick what your app actually needs.
oauthClients.scopeOfflineHint: refresh tokens — keep users signed in past 1h
oauthClients.credentialsDescription: Copy now. The client_secret will not be shown again — store it as {envName} in your app's deploy env.

create.title: Create your status page
create.lede: Atlassian Statuspage-style real-time status board. Define your components, hook up HTTP health checks, publish at status.sentroy.com/p/{slug}.
create.slugHint: Lowercase letters, digits and hyphens. Used in the public URL — cannot be changed afterwards.

overview.embedHint: Add the snippet below to your site to display a live status badge. Origin must be in the allow-list (Settings → Allowed embed origins).
overview.embedDisabled: Subscribers + embed disabled in Settings — enable to use this widget.

components.emptyBody: Components group your checks into user-facing services ("API", "Database", "Web App"). Their status is derived from underlying checks.
components.descriptionPlaceholder: Short description shown on public page (optional)
components.groupHint: Components sharing the same group appear together on the public page.
components.visibleHint: Hide to keep this component for internal monitoring only.
components.deleteConfirmDescription: Its checks and their probe history will be removed permanently.

checks.emptyBody: Checks define how a component's health is verified — HTTP probe URL + interval or TCP socket connect. Component status is derived from worst severity of its checks.

typeHint.http: Send HTTP GET/POST/HEAD to the URL; verify status code and optional body match.
typeHint.tcp: Attempt a TCP 3-way handshake on host:port — for non-HTTP services like PostgreSQL, Redis, SMTP, SSH.

checks.editTypeLocked: Type can't be changed after creation — delete and recreate.
checks.intervalHint: How often to probe. Min 30, max 3600.
checks.degradedHint: Probes slower than this are marked degraded (not down). Threshold > timeout makes no sense.
checks.insecureHint: Allow self-signed certificates. Only use in dev.
checks.enabledHint: When off, worker skips this check.
checks.restartSectionHint: Bind a restart target so the worker fires it after N consecutive downs. Create targets under the Restart targets tab.
checks.restartTargetHint: Configure restart targets in the Restart targets tab first.
checks.deleteConfirmDescription: Probe history will be removed permanently.

incidents.description: Track outages and degradations — manually open one, or let the worker auto-detect sustained downtime. Post timeline updates so users know what's happening.
incidents.emptyBody: Open an incident when something breaks, or let the worker auto-detect after 3 consecutive check failures.
incidents.createDialogDescription: Manual incident. Auto-detected ones appear when the worker sees sustained failures.
incidents.componentsEmpty: Add components first to associate them with this incident.
incidents.initialUpdatePlaceholder: We're investigating reports of slow mail delivery from our EU region.
incidents.initialUpdateHint: Appears as the first entry in the timeline. Defaults to a generic 'investigating' message if blank.
incidents.postUpdateBodyPlaceholder: We've identified the root cause: a misconfigured DNS record. Rolling out fix.
incidents.deleteConfirmDescription: Permanently remove this incident and all its updates. Use only for test/spam entries — for actual resolved incidents, leave them in history.
incidents.postmortemHint: Root cause + actions taken + prevention. Markdown supported. Shown on the public incident detail.
incidents.postmortemBodyPlaceholder:
## Summary
Mail send queue throttled for 32 minutes.

## Root cause
Upstream SMTP provider downgraded our IP reputation.

## Actions
- Failed over to backup provider
- Added reputation monitoring alarm

maintenance.description: Schedule planned downtime windows. Affected components show as 'maintenance' on the public page and uptime is not penalized.
maintenance.emptyBody: Plan announced downtime in advance — subscribers get notified, public page shows a banner, affected components are exempted from uptime stats.
maintenance.dialogDescription: Window times are local; converted to UTC on save.
maintenance.descriptionPlaceholder: Migrating to MongoDB 7. Read-only mode for ~30 minutes.
maintenance.componentsEmpty: Add components first to associate them with this window.
maintenance.deleteConfirmDescription: Permanently remove this maintenance window. Use only for mistakenly created entries.

audit.description: Every CRUD action on this status page — components, checks, incidents, maintenance windows, subscribers, restart targets — is recorded here for compliance + debugging.
audit.emptyBody: As soon as you (or the worker) make changes, they appear here in reverse chronological order.

subscribers.description: Verified email + webhook subscribers receiving notifications from your public page.
subscribers.emptyBody: When visitors subscribe from the public page, they'll appear here after confirming via email. Webhook subscribers (POST endpoints) appear immediately.
subscribers.editComponentsHint: Empty selection = notify for any component.
subscribers.deleteConfirmDescription: Permanently remove this subscriber. They won't receive any further notifications.
subscribers.deliveriesDialogDescription: Per-subscriber dispatch results across email/webhook/telegram for the last 30 days. Includes error messages and HTTP status for failures.
subscribers.deliveriesEmpty: No delivery records yet. After the next incident update, deliveries will appear here.
subscribers.importDialogDescription: Header row required. type must be email. topics and components are separated with semicolons (;). Imported subscribers are created as verified — opt-in chain is on you.

targets.description: Endpoints the worker calls to recover a failing check — your own /restart route (HTTP), a remote server command (SSH), or a Coolify resource redeploy.

typeHint.http: Worker POSTs/GETs your URL when the threshold is hit. Optional auth header encrypted.
typeHint.ssh: Worker SSH's into a remote host with your private key and runs a single command.
typeHint.coolify: Worker calls Coolify's /api/v1/deploy with your API token to redeploy the resource.

targets.editTypeLocked: Type can't change after creation. Delete and recreate to switch.
targets.sshKeySectionHint: OpenSSH or PEM. Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted. Worker decrypts at trigger time; plaintext never leaves the dashboard.
targets.sshPassphrasePlaceholder: Leave blank if key has no passphrase
targets.sshCommandHint: Single command — pipelines allowed but discouraged. Exit code 0 = success.
targets.sshFieldsRequired: Host, username, private key and command are all required
targets.sshCoolifyNoEdit: Edit for SSH/Coolify targets isn't supported in v1 — delete and recreate to rotate credentials.
targets.coolifyTokenSectionHint: Create in Coolify Keys & Tokens with deploy scope. Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted.
targets.coolifyFieldsRequired: Base URL, API token and resource UUID are all required
targets.emptyBody: Create an HTTP target that points to your service's restart endpoint. Bind it to a check, and the worker will hit it after N consecutive failures.
targets.dialogDescription: HTTP target. SSH and Coolify built-in coming in a later phase.
targets.authSectionHint: Stored AES-256-GCM encrypted. Worker decrypts at trigger time and adds it as the named header.
targets.authHeaderValueRotateLabel: New value (leave blank to keep existing)
targets.bodyHint: Sent only on POST. Free-form text — JSON, form-urlencoded, etc.
targets.deleteConfirmDescription: Bound checks will be unlinked. History stays.

testFireConfirmDescription.http: Sends a live HTTP request to the restart endpoint once. The service may go through a restart cycle.
testFireConfirmDescription.ssh: Connects via SSH to the remote host and runs the command live (`docker restart …` etc.). May affect a production service.
testFireConfirmDescription.coolify: Sends a live deploy webhook to Coolify. The resource is rebuilt and restarted — brief downtime possible.

settings.nameHint: Shown in your dashboard list. Public display name is set under Branding.
settings.enabledHint: When disabled, the public page returns 404. Existing data is preserved.
settings.displayNameHint: Shown in the page header and meta title.
settings.logoLinkUrlHint: When set, clicking the logo on the public page opens this URL in a new tab. Leave empty to keep the logo non-clickable.
settings.subscribersHint: Show "Subscribe to updates" button on the public page (email + webhook). When off, embed widget is also unavailable.
settings.embedOriginsHint: Origins permitted to embed the status widget via iframe. Leave empty to disable embedding.
settings.deleteHint: Permanently delete the page along with all components, checks, probe history, incidents, maintenances, subscribers, and restart targets. Cannot be undone.
settings.deleteConfirmDescription: Type the slug to confirm. All data will be permanently removed.
